_doc: Hand-editable target measure for emission-v1 diversity. Uniform over feasible cells (type × morph_cluster × palette_flavor × render_style) by default. SCHEMA (as the loader emission.cells.TargetMeasure actually parses it — verified campaign-2 preflight): each weight_overrides entry is {"match": {axis: [values...]}, "weight": M} and multiplies the base weight (1.0) of every cell whose value on each named axis is in that axis's listed set. A flat {"fractal_type": [...], "multiplier": M} form is SILENTLY IGNORED (weight() reads ov['match'] / ov['weight']), degenerating the measure to uniform. attempt_cap evicts a cell from the target support after that many colorize attempts with zero fills. softmax_temp controls how sharply the deficit-max option is preferred (small → argmax, large → uniform); deficits are range-normalized before softmax so this is scale-free.
_weights_note: First hand-placed allocation order, post campaign-1 blind read (2026-07-20). Rationale: julia families up-weighted on looks-scarcity (the proven vein, under-produced; per-twin census ordering NOT baked in — bug-survivor rates, re-measured at campaign 2, differentiate then if warranted); mandelbrot slight up (best c-plane family); mb3/mb5 slightly down; mb4 token weight, not zero (worst to humans, but nonzero + attempt caps lets the fixed dup metric prove or disprove a yield change). Only relative size matters. Implied per-type shares IF the projection normalizes types independently of cluster count: each julia twin ~19% (julia total ~77%), mandelbrot ~9%, mb3/mb5 ~6% each, mb4 ~2%.
_phoenix_note: Phoenix entered the library at library_intake_2 (2026-07-21, t_good 0.45): 196 varied (grid) + 41 classic (Ushiki) distinct clusters. Classic phoenix separates CLEANLY in morph space (its 41 clusters phoenix#196..236 share zero members with the varied grid clusters), so a morph_cluster override addresses it precisely — the ONLY way to shape classic vs varied within one fractal_type. The classic override below (weight 0.35) yields a ~1.5% classic-phoenix share of the JOINT measure (near the ~2% target; the joint share depends on all weights + live cluster counts, re-tune at emission). CAVEAT: varied phoenix carries NO type-weight, so at 196 clusters it sits at ~20% of the joint measure — likely too high for a narrow log-spiral motif (docs/findings/phoenix_grid_labels.md); a separate fractal_type:['phoenix'] down-weight is the knob for that, deliberately NOT added here (this edit is scoped to the classic-share stanza only). The morph_cluster ids are unstable across re-intake (incremental-medoid order), so re-derive them if the library is re-clustered.
